channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This Google Maps ‘safety’ feature could actually make roads more dangerous
item.description: Picture this: You’re driving on a crowded highway, preparing to change lanes and pass a tractor-trailer. As you check your mirrors, a loud chime on your car’s infotainment screen rings out. It’s Google Maps, asking whether a stalled car is still on the shoulder, as other drivers have reported.
item.dc:creator: David Zipper, Fast Company

item.title: How Elon Musk $97.4 billion bid complicates matters for OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has soundly dismissed a $97.4 billion takeover bid backed by rival Elon Musk, but the unsolicited offer is likely to add complications to Altman’s push to transform the maker of ChatGPT into a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Thalia Beaty And Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An AI agent could help you buy your next car
item.description: Capital One has launched an AI agent designed to help customers with one of the more difficult and confusing purchase decisions: buying a car.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Upholding Cybersecurity: Why the Trump Administration Should Support the Final Executive Order
item.description: A critical examination of the importance of upholding the final cybersecurity executive order, despite partisan divides and the transition to a new administration.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 could get a major battery upgrade that makes it worth waiting for
item.description: A new report claims the Samsung Galaxy S26 could get silicon carbon batteries, making it worth holding fire on grabbing the new Galaxy S25 if you want huge battery capacities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supermarket Simulator Pro is no more as a number of 'spam' games are removed from the PS Store
item.description: It looks like Sony may have quietly removed a number of "spam" shovelware games from its PlayStation Store.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple’s security patch highlights the growing security threat
item.description: Apple’s platforms may be more secure by design than others, but that doesn’t make them invulnerable to attack. That’s why every user should install the company’s latest security patch — it fixes a hole Apple says may already have been in active use.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Workday Unveils New Agent System, Expands Role-Based AI Strategy
item.description: Workday announces new agent system and expands role-based AI strategy to manage AI agents and third-party agents in one place.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: South Bay tech campus $15 million deal points to feeble office market
item.description: A big tech campus in Sunnyvale has been bought for about $15 million in a fresh sign of a frail Bay Area office market.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft to rejig Office-Teams cost to avoid EU fine
item.description: Microsoft is reportedly working on increasing the price difference between its Microsoft 365 suite bundled with Teams and the standalone Teams application in a bid to avoid an antitrust fine from the EU.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trasformazione digitale: quando il CIO è un knowledge manager che spezza i silos aziendali
item.description: I CIO si trovano in una posizione unica per tracciare la strada verso la crescita e l’innovazione della loro azienda, hanno scritto [in inglese] gli analisti della società di consulenza Kearney.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This website combines Wikipedia and TikTok to fight doomscrolling
item.description: “Insane project idea: all of wikipedia on a single, scrollable page,” Patina Systems founder Tyler Angert posted on X earlier this month. “Even better, an infinitely scrolling Wikipedia page based on whatever you are interested in next?” replied Bloomberg Beta VC James Cham. “WikiTok,” added Angert.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: ESA’s space telescope Euclid discovers ‘astonishing' Einstein ring
item.description: The European Space Agency's Euclid space telescope was going through preliminary tests in September 2023 when it captured images of an Einstein Ring in a nearby galaxy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: C++, Go, and Rust gaining popularity – Tiobe
item.description: With Tiobe’s February 2025 rankings, the publisher of the monthly index of programming language popularity notes that fast programming languages such as C++, Go, and Rust are gaining ground.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What you need to know about Python 3.14’s faster interpreter
item.description: Python 3.14, due out later this year, is set to receive a new type of interpreter that can boost performance by up to 30% with no changes to existing code. The CPython 3.14 change log describes the feature as “a new type of interpreter based on tail calls.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ye's X account deleted after antisemitic posts
item.description: Ye, the rapper formerly known as Kanye West, has left the social media platform X after a days-long posting spree that included multiple antisemitic comments.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation

item.title: Visual Studio Code unveils Copilot code editing suggestions
item.description: A just-released update of Visual Studio Code, version 1.97, introduces a preview of Copilot Next Edit Suggestions, an AI-powered feature that predicts the next edit the user wants to make.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: UK orders Apple to let it access everyone’s encrypted data
item.description: In its limited wisdom, the deeply unpopular UK government has decided to break privacy for the entire world, slamming Apple with a top secret order that demands blanket access to personal data. Apple must create a “back door” to enable surveillance, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AWS plans to outspend Microsoft and Google on AI infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) intends to invest $100 billion in ramping up infrastructure for AI cloud services this year, surpassing the spending plans of rivals Microsoft and Google.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Half of C-Suite leaders likely to leave in two years, 27% in six months
item.description: A new Gartner survey of more than 200 C-level executives found 56% are “extremely likely” to quit their current jobs over the next two years — and 27% plan to exit within the next six months.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Oracle maintains hold on JavaScript trademark
item.description: Oracle, under external pressure to yield control over the JavaScript trademark, has instead filed a motion for dismissal of part of a petition to cancel the trademark.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Beware of fake Reddit solutions delivering dangerous malware
item.description: Bad actors are now mimicking Reddit to spread malware that can steal personal information. CyberGuy shares what you need to know about fake Reddit pages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data
item.description: A Treasury official says DOGE team members have “read-only” access to the data from its payment systems as part of an “operational efficiency assessment." The post DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hacked on social? Here’s what to do next
item.description: Spambots, data thieves, and other bad actors run rampant online — more so now than ever before. Here's what to do if one gets ahold of you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
